entries.title: Allies fear they are tied to an erratic U.S. and now have nowhere to turn
President Trump told the WSJ that Iran has until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or he will destroy the country’s bridges and power plants. Read more in today’s WSJ politics newsletter:
For nearly two days, injured and alone, a U.S. aviator hid in a remote mountain crevice as Iranian forces and militias closed in on him
President Trump threatened to destroy all of Iran’s power plants if the country’s leaders don’t agree to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday evening, ratcheting up pressure on Tehran
President Trump’s repeated declarations that the Iran war is nearly over are colliding with the gritty battlefield reality, some U.S. officials and analysts say
President Trump is reshaping how the federal government collects and spends money, while doing little to rein in federal deficits
Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito was taken to a hospital and given fluids for dehydration last month after attending a dinner in his honor in Philadelphia
President Trump released a summary of his budget proposal for the 2027 fiscal year, seeking $1.5 trillion in defense spending, by far the largest dollar amount in modern history
Democratic Senators Call on Trump to Block Chinese Automakers From Manufacturing in U.S.
Attorney General Pam Bondi was always on the ropes but thought she was finally on safer footing. She wasn’t
Democratic Colorado Gov. Jared Polis is taking flak from Democrats and moderate Republicans—but praise from the right—for seeming to bow to President Trump’s demand to grant clemency to Tina Peters
Retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman is a Democrat running for Senate in solidly Republican Florida
President Trump released mock-up images of a gleaming glass tower emblazoned with his last name that will serve as his presidential library once he leaves office
Missouri Republican Rep. Sam Graves, chairman of the House Committee on Transportation, is pulling his plans to seek re-election
President Trump has told associates in recent days that he wants to avoid a protracted war in Iran and that he hopes to bring the conflict to an end in the coming weeks
Democrat Emily Gregory beat a Trump-backed Republican in a special election for the Florida state house district that includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ousted the Army’s top general Thursday, the latest top military leader removed in a Pentagon that has seen a purge of its top ranks under the Trump administration
President Trump sought to reassure skeptical Americans that the war in Iran is in the national interest, arguing that the operation was necessary to decimate a regime threatening the U.S.
The drone having its moment in the Middle East conflict was designed by the U.S. military itself, using reverse-engineered Iranian technology
President Trump is weighing a military operation to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of uranium from Iran, a complex mission that would likely put U.S. forces inside the country for days or longer

Investigators searched the office of a FEMA contractor as they investigate a network of aides put in place by former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski, according to people familiar with the matter
